title: Purged Group-Time CV With Fold-Safe Feature Rebuild
summary: Your best code still learns several preprocessors and same-lap/context statistics on concatenated data, which can make random StratifiedKFold optimistic for a panel problem with repeated race-driver trajectories.
rationale: Your best code still learns several preprocessors and same-lap/context statistics on concatenated data, which can make random StratifiedKFold optimistic for a panel problem with repeated race-driver trajectories. Group-aware and time-aware splits with a gap are the main validation trap to test before trusting small CV gains.
implementation_hint: Use groups such as `(Year, Race)` or `(Year, Race, Driver)` and compare `GroupKFold`, leave-one-race-out, and chronological `TimeSeriesSplit(gap=1-2 laps)` on rows sorted within race. Recompute same-lap means, frequency/count encodings, bins, and any TE maps inside each fold only; treat any CV drop paired with stabler public LB as a positive signal.
expected_effect: May reduce local CV slightly but should improve leaderboard correlation and prevent over-selecting leaky feature variants.
risk: If the test set is IID across races, stricter CV may be too pessimistic and slow iteration without improving score.
